terms.definition: The extent to which interacting parts of a system hold together in stable, synergistic patterns that permit effective collective action.
A weighted composite index of multiple social indicators intended to quantify systemic coherence on a 0–1 scale.
terms.first_use_section: Formal Statement of the Coherence Factor χ

terms.definition: Any empirical metric rescaled to the 0–1 interval so that heterogeneous variables can be combined in a single index.
The provisional value of χ below which the model predicts systemic fragmentation.
terms.first_use_section: Formal Statement of the Coherence Factor χ

terms.definition: Alignment of attention or behaviour produced by centralized broadcast media rather than by local relational bonds.
Alignment emerging from direct interpersonal relationships and shared local practices.
A system state in which redundant support structures have been removed, reducing error-tolerance and increasing fragility to shocks.
An abrupt, non-linear shift from one systemic equilibrium to another once a control parameter crosses a critical value.
